properties.remark: WESH CHANNEL 2 ORLANDO REPORTED TREES BLOWN DOWN ON TOP OF CARS AND POWER LINES DOWN, KNOCKING OUT POWER FOR SEVERAL BLOCKS.

WFTV CHANNEL 9 ORLANDO REPORTED AN EMPTY CONSTRUCTION TRAILER PARKED ON THE SIDE OF THE ROAD OVERTURNED ON NARCOOSEE ROAD NEAR SR 417 IN FRONT OF LAKE NONA HIGH SCHOOL IN ORLANDO. TIME ESTIMATED FROM RADAR.
NEWLY PLANTED LARGE PALM TREES TOPPLED, FENCES KNOCKED OVER, DAMAGE TO SEVERAL POOL SCREEN ENCLOSURES AT BRUNOT CIRCLE, VIERA FL. WIND ESTIMATED 50-60 MPH.
YOUNG STURDY TREE SNAPPED IN HALF. HEAVY GLASS DOORS BLOWN OPEN BACKWARDS AGAINST THE HINGES WITH WELL CONTRUCTED ROOF CREAKING AT THE VIERA SCHOOL BOARD BUILDING. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
YOUNG STURDY TREE SNAPPED IN HALF. HEAVY GLASS DOORS BLOWN OPEN BACKWARDS AGAINST THE HINGES WITH WELL CONTRUCTED ROOF CREAKING AT THE VIERA SCHOOL BOARD BUILDING. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
DAMAGE TO SCREEN PORCH OF A HOUSE IN THE VIERA AREA. POSSIBLE TORNADO
EF0 TORNADO CONFIRMED WITH WINDS ESTIMATED 65-75 MPH AND A PATH LENGTH ESTIMATED AROUND 6 MILES FROM NEAR JACQUES DRIVE IN VIERA TO DEL SOL DRIVE ON MERRITT ISLAND
TREES TOPPLED AND SNAPPED, MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO 6 HOMES NEAR US1 AND VIERA BLVD. THIS DAMAGE IS ASSOCIATED WITH THE TORNADO THAT BEGAN AT 0738 AM 5 S OF ROCKLEDGE.
TREES TOPPLED AND SNAPPED, MINOR STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO 6 HOMES NEAR US1 AND VIERA BLVD. DAMAGE FROM EF0 TORNADO.
NUMEROUS TREES TOPPLED AND SNAPPED, MINOR DAMAGE TO SEVERAL HOMES AND POOL SCREEN ENCLOSURES. THIS DAMAGE IS ASSOCIATED WITH THE TORNADO THAT BEGAN AT 0738 AM 5 S OF ROCKLEDGE.
STRUCTURAL DAMAGE TO A TILE ROOF. POSSIBLE TORNADO.
MINOR DAMAGE TO SEVERAL UNITS OF AN APARTMENT BUILDING AND VEHICLE DAMAGE NEAR A1A AND 36TH STREET SOUTH. DAMAGE FROM EF0 TORNADO.

EF0 TORNADO WAS CONFIRMED WITH PATH LENGTH OF 2.4 MILES FROM SOUTHWEST OF I-95 AND EAU GALLIE INTERSECTION TO SOUTHWEST OF AURORA AND WICKHAM ROAD INTERSECTION. MAXIMUM ESTIMATED WINDS OF 60 MPH BLEW DOWN NUMEROUS TREE BRANCHES...TOPPLED ONE LARGE TREE...BLEW FENCES OVER...AND DID MINOR DAMAGE TO TWO HOMES. THIS IS AN UPDATE TO THE STORM REPORT FROM 606 PM 3/28/10.
POSSIBLE TORNADO ON CYPRESS BEND CIRCLE OFF JOHN RODES BLVD. TREE BRANCHES BROKE SEVERAL WINDOWS OF A RESIDENCE.
TREE ON HOUSE AT EAST WASHINGTON STREET
DOWNED POWER LINES

STRONG WINDS BLEW ARM OF A CRANE INTO POWER LINES, TAKING THEM DOWN. EVENT OCCURRED AT A FLORIDA DOT CONSTRUCTION SITE ON SOUTH SIDE OF VERO BEACH, SOUTH OF THE INTERSECTION OF 4TH STREET AND U.S. 1. REPORT FROM SCRIPPS TREASURE COAST NEWSPAPER.

LINES DOWN ON ROAD. ROAD BLOCKED.

TREE BRANCH DOWN IN MONTVERDE, OTHERWISE, NO DAMAGE REPORTED. INFORMATION RELAYED BY WFTV CHANNEL 9.

WIND DAMAGE TO SEVERAL MOBILE HOMES WITHIN COLONIAL VILLAGE MOBILE HOME PARK. ONE CARPORT DESTROYED, SIDING AND GUTERS BLOWN OFF, SEVERAL TREES KNOCKED DOWN. INFORMATION RELAYED FROM CF NEWS 13.

TREES DOWN AND CHICK-FIL-A SIGN DOWN NEAR WAL MART AT HOLLYWOOD BLVD. AND PALM BAY RD.
WIND GUST OF 51 KNOTS REPORTED AT REEDY LAKE
SIGNIFICANT ROOF DAMAGE TO ONE HOME NEAR SAXON DRIVE AND SANDPIPER WAY. MODERATE DAMAGE TO ROOFS OF SURROUNDING HOMES.

EFO TOUCHDOWN NEAR SANDPIPER AVENUE AND SAXON DRIVE. EXTENSIVE ROOF DAMAGE INCLUDING ONE HOME HAVING A QUARTER OF THEIR ROOF RIPPED OFF. A SURFER SPOTTED INSULATION BEING BLOWN TO THE OCEAN ABOUT 1/2 MILE AWAY. PLYWOOD WAS RIPPED OFF TRUSSES AND A POLICE CAR WAS ALSO SPUN AROUND.

A WATERSPOUT WAS SPOTTED TO HAVE MOVED ONSHORE AT RIVERSIDE LANDING CONDOMINIUMS THEN MOVED NORTHWARD INTO CAMERONS MARINA CAUSING A BOAT ON LAND TO BE OVERTURNED. FROM HERE, IT CONTINUED TO MOVE NORTH TO THE 1800 BLOCK OF RIVERSIDE DRIVE WHERE IT DISSIPATED THEREAFTER.

VOLUSIA COUNTY EVAC AMBULANCE REPORTED A POSSIBLE BRIEF TORNADO TOUCHDOWN IN A WOODED AREA WEST OF I-95 AND SOUTH OF SR 44.
